pending.body: finding_summary: The Codex fast-mode suffix feature is still unimplemented. `codex_model_and_reasoning_effort()` only strips reasoning suffixes (`-xhigh`, `-high`, `-medium`, `-low`) and never recognizes `-fast`, so `codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh-fast` currently flows through `codex_new_session_args()` / `codex_resume_args()` as `--model gpt-5.4-xhigh-fast` with no fast-mode flag. That means the bead's core acceptance criteria fail for both fresh and resumed Codex invocations. severity: high. affected_files: src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1319-1399; tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs:751-822, 890-939, 1020-1082. remediation_hint: Add a Codex model parser that peels an optional `-fast` suffix in addition to reasoning effort, use it in both argument builders, and add command-construction tests for `...-fast` and non-fast variants.

The plan_and_implement stage hallucinated its implementation. Zero lines of source code were changed between the last merged PR (fa1bae34) and HEAD. The artifact claims completion of 4 steps with 3 named tests and passing cargo test/clippy/nix build, but none of these tests exist and no code was written. The implementation must actually be performed: (1) extend codex_model_and_reasoning_effort() in src/adapters/process_backend.rs to detect and strip a trailing -fast suffix from the model ID, (2) wire the fast mode flag into codex_new_session_args() and codex_resume_session_args() as -c service_tier="fast", (3) add regression tests covering model ID parsing with -fast suffix for standard and spark variants, (4) ensure the reasoning effort suffix parsing still works when combined with -fast (e.g. gpt-5.4-xhigh-fast should parse as model=gpt-5.4, effort=xhigh, fast=true).

status_summary: running: final review restart round 3 -> Plan and Implement
